title: Histotechnologists
description: Apply knowledge of health and disease causes to evaluate new laboratory techniques and procedures to examine tissue samples. Process and prepare histological slides from tissue sections for microscopic examination and diagnosis by pathologists. May solve technical or instrument problems or assist with research studies.

tasks.text: Embed tissue specimens into paraffin wax blocks, or infiltrate tissue specimens with wax.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Cut sections of body tissues for microscopic examination, using microtomes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Stain tissue specimens with dyes or other chemicals to make cell details visible under microscopes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Compile materials for distribution to pathologists, such as surgical working drafts, requisitions, and slides.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Compile and maintain records of preventive maintenance and instrument performance checks according to schedule and regulations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform tests by following physician instructions.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Operate computerized laboratory equipment to dehydrate, decalcify, or microincinerate tissue samples.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare substances, such as reagents and dilution, and stains for histological specimens according to protocols.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Resolve problems with laboratory equipment and instruments, such as microscopes, mass spectrometers, microtomes, immunostainers, tissue processors, embedding centers, and water baths.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Examine slides under microscopes to ensure tissue preparation meets laboratory requirements.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare or use prepared tissue specimens for teaching, research or diagnostic purposes.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform procedures associated with histochemistry to prepare specimens for immunofluorescence or microscopy.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Identify tissue structures or cell components to be used in the diagnosis, prevention, or treatment of diseases.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform electron microscopy or mass spectrometry to analyze specimens.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
